---
title: "Poisson Demo"
author: "Ronnie Bailey-Steinitz"
date: "2026-07-10"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}

library(tidyverse)

# Change this number
lambda <- 1

# Possible counts
counts <- 0:40

# Poisson probabilities
pois_data <- tibble(
  count = counts,
  probability = dpois(counts, lambda = lambda)
)

# Plot
ggplot(pois_data, aes(x = count, y = probability)) +
  geom_col(fill = "turquoise3") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = lambda,
             linetype = "solid",
             color = "red", size = 1) + # the mean / lambda
  labs(
    title = paste("Poisson Distribution (λ =", lambda, ")"),
    x = "Number of events",
    y = "Probability"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

# The red line marks λ, the expected (average) number of events. As λ increases, the whole distribution shifts to the right and spreads out.
```

```{r}
# Values of lambda to compare
lambdas <- c(1, 2, 4, 8, 10)

# Possible counts
counts <- 0:30

# Create dataset
pois_data2 <- expand.grid(
  count = counts,
  lambda = lambdas
) %>%
  mutate(
    probability = dpois(count, lambda)
  )

# Plot
ggplot(pois_data2,
       aes(x = count,
           y = probability,
           color = factor(lambda))) +
  geom_line(size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of events",
    y = "Probability",
    color = expression(lambda)
  ) +
  theme_bw()

ggplot(pois_data2,
       aes(count, probability)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = as.factor(lambda))) +
  facet_wrap(~lambda, nrow = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "Number of events",
    y = "Probability",
    fill = expression(lambda)
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```
